text.title: Solo Bug Triage Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Solo Bug Triage Rubric. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Triage uses ONLY 5 questions: severity, scope, frequency, reachability, workaround.
rule.rationale: Triage uses ONLY 5 questions: severity, scope, frequency, reachability, workaround.

rule.statement: Output is exactly one of: drop-everything / next-sprint / accept.
rule.rationale: Output is exactly one of: drop-everything / next-sprint / accept.

rule.statement: Every triaged bug has a named owner (handle), never 'team'.
rule.rationale: Every triaged bug has a named owner (handle), never 'team'.

rule.statement: Accepted bugs carry a retest threshold (e.g. 'if frequency >5/wk, escalate').
rule.rationale: Accepted bugs carry a retest threshold (e.g. 'if frequency >5/wk, escalate').

rule.statement: Accepted bugs decay after 30 days; auto-re-open for re-triage.
rule.rationale: Accepted bugs decay after 30 days; auto-re-open for re-triage.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-solo-bug-triage-rubric.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
